title: eremophila pusilliflora
intro: Eremophila pusilliflora, also known as the smallflower emu bush, is a small shrub native to Western Australia and is a member of the figwort family. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, burns, and skin irritations. Eremophila pusilliflora has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
